acceptance.label: Maintainers can inspect per-view exposure, invocation counts, unique task usage, and latency from durable logs
The plan yields an explicit promote, keep-experimental, or remove decision for each newly added view
Unused or low-value views do not remain permanently exposed by default

payload.summary: Close the loop on registry-backed query views. Add or reuse analysis surfaces over query and MCP logs so maintainers can answer which views are exposed, which are used, how often, by how many tasks, with what latency, and whether fallback behavior suggests the view actually helped. Use those measurements to promote a small set of useful views, keep marginal ones experimental, and remove dead or misleading surfaces instead of accumulating permanent API clutter.
payload.title: Milestone 5: Add adoption review, prune dead views, and promote survivors
